company.name: Brigade Electronics
company.description: Global manufacturer of vehicle safety systems for commercial vehicles and machinery — cameras, sensors, radar, AI vision, and white-sound reversing alarms

primary.name: Safety Yellow
primary.usage: Primary brand colour, CTAs, safety highlights, hazard markers

secondary.name: Industrial Black
secondary.usage: Headers, body text, document backgrounds, contrast against yellow

accent.name: Hi-Vis Red
accent.usage: Critical alerts, danger indicators, important statistics

heading.usage: H1, H2, H3 headlines — strong industrial feel

mono.usage: Product codes, model numbers, technical specifications

brand_voice.tone: Plain-spoken expert — talks to operators, not boardrooms
brand_voice.style: Outcome-focused: lives saved, accidents prevented, compliance achieved

key_messages: Eliminate vehicle blind spots and prevent collisions
Patented bbs-tek white-sound — locatable, less noise pollution
AI vision detection that distinguishes humans from objects
Compliant with FORS, CLOCS, DVS Progressive Safe System
Trusted globally by transport, construction, and emergency services
value_propositions: Provide indisputable video evidence for incidents
Engineered for harsh environments — built to last

full.usage: Primary logo for documents, websites, vehicle decals

mark.usage: Icon/mark only for product labels, social media, favicons

imagery.style: Real working environments — HGVs, plant, refuse trucks, depots
imagery.photography: Drivers and operators in-situ, equipment installed on real vehicles, on-the-job action shots
imagery.illustrations: Technical diagrams of camera angles, blind-spot zones, sound directionality

do: Use safety yellow (#FFD200) and industrial black (#1A1A1A) as primary pairing
Lead with concrete safety outcomes (lives saved, accidents prevented)
Cite compliance standards by name (FORS, CLOCS, DVS, BS EN 7010)
Show real vehicles and operators, not stock office photography
Use plain, direct language — no jargon, no marketing fluff
Reference actual product codes (Backeye 360, bbs-tek, Backsense) where appropriate
dont: Don't use pastel or muted colour palettes — high contrast is the brand
Don't use boardroom/financial imagery — Brigade speaks to operations
Don't make claims without evidence — link to test data or case studies
Don't undersell the safety stakes — this product prevents fatalities
Don't use Finabeo-style governance/transformation language — it's not the audience
Don't substitute fonts other than Roboto / Roboto Condensed without approval

metadata.notes: Based on public brigade-electronics.com observation. Awaiting confirmation of actual brand assets (exact Pantone, logo SVGs, font licensing). Colour palette inferred from the safety-yellow/black industrial aesthetic visible on the website and product photography.
next_steps: Confirm exact yellow Pantone with Brigade brand team
Provide official logo SVGs in multiple variants
Confirm font licensing for Roboto Condensed in commercial materials
Validate compliance terminology and product names with product team
